channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Medicare costs are rising in 2026. Here's how to save during open enrollment
item.description: For some, a reduction in benefits could be coming as soon as next year — along with a bigger price tag.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Aldrich, KREX 5

item.title: US Embassy issues warning to Americans in Trinidad and Tobago as tensions with Venezuela escalate
item.description: PORT-OF-SPAIN, Trinidad (AP) — The U.S. Embassy in Trinidad and Tobago cautioned Americans on Saturday to stay away from American government facilities on the twin-island nation. It was an unusual warning that came as tensions grow between the United States and Venezuela over deadly U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANSELM GIBBS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US will send survivors of strike on suspected drug vessel back to Ecuador and Colombia, Trump says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The two survivors of an American military strike on a suspected drug-carrying vessel in the Caribbean will be sent to Ecuador and Colombia, their home countries, President Donald Trump said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Legally blind Ohio artist says she can't drive, but she can tattoo
item.description: Nicole Morrone was born legally blind, but she still creates detailed, colorful artwork -- on her clients' skin.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Cloutier, WGN 9

item.title: Alaska storm damage so bad many evacuees won’t go home for at least 18 months, governor says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Damage to remote Alaska villages hammered by flooding last weekend is so extreme that many of the more than 2,000 people displaced won’t be able to return to their homes for at least 18 months, Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: With no takers yet, White House meets with colleges still weighing an agreement with Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The five universities that were still weighing President Donald Trump’s higher-education compact were asked to join a White House call Friday to discuss the proposed deal.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: National Guard intervenes as suspect swings foot-long kitchen knife in violent DC street brawl
item.description: National Guard members intervened in a chaotic street fight near Nationals Park Wednesday that ended with one man being stabbed with a large kitchen knife.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: News stories show that reporters may have left the Pentagon, but they haven't stopped working
item.description: Two days after dozens of journalists left their desks at the Pentagon behind rather than agree to government-imposed rules on how they report about the U.S. military, it's apparent they haven't stopped working. Reporters have relied on sources to break and add nuance to stories about U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deployment of National Guard to Chicago
item.description: The Trump administration has asked the Supreme Court to let it deploy the National Guard to the Chicago area amid President Trump’s push to send military troops into Democratic-led cities.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kevin Federline says his sons with Britney Spears are the reason for his new memoir
item.description: Kevin Federline says concern for his two sons with Britney Spears long kept him from telling his story, and those same concerns are the reason he's telling it now that they're men.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LA county reaches tentative $828M agreement to settle 400 more claims of sexual abuse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles County has reached a tentative $828 million agreement to settle more than 400 additional sexual abuse claims against county employees, months after approving the largest sexual abuse settlement in U.S. history, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tensions rise ahead of 'No Kings' protests: What to know
item.description: Tensions have risen between critics and allies of the Trump administration as the country approaches the second "No Kings Day" of 2025.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, NewsNation

item.title: Arkansas boy with terminal illness finds strength in show ring
item.description: Ledger Lamkin started showing animals after watching his sisters compete and quickly found his passion.
item.dc:creator: Caden Michaels, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump's approval shifted after the ceasefire in Gaza, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More U.S. adults support President Donald Trump's handling of the conflict in Israel after he brokered a ceasefire deal in Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds — but his approval ratings on domestic issues remain weak.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother says alleged stalker who killed her daughter should be tried as an adult
item.description: The mother of Maria Niotis calls for justice after her teenage daughter and friend Isabella Salas died in a New Jersey crash involving an alleged juvenile stalker facing murder charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ty Dolla $ign returns with ‘Tycoon,’ his first solo album in five years
item.description: The inventive R&B singer has just released his fifth studio album, his first full-length solo release in five years.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah volunteer heads to Alaska after storm devastates coastal villages
item.description: A volunteer with American Red Cross of Utah is heading to Anchorage, Alaska, to support victims of Typhoon Halong, which caused severe flooding and displaced thousands along Alaska's western coastline, according to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: 78-year-old man missing in Oregon may have fallen in hole; police ask for help
item.description: In a phone call with his family, Thomas Weightman said he had fallen into a hole and did not know where he was.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: South Carolina schedules execution for man who taunted police with message in victim's blood
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina inmate who killed a man, burned his eyes with cigarettes and then painted “catch me if u can” on the wall with the victim's blood more than 20 years ago has been scheduled to be executed next month.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione’s lawyers say Trump admin is making him political pawn
item.description: Luigi Mangione's defense team issued a letter to the judge arguing the Trump admin is using him as a political pawn to further its agenda.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Father pleads guilty to murder of missing 7-month-old son in Southern California
item.description: RIVERSIDE, Calif. (AP) — The father of a 7-month-old boy missing in Southern California pleaded guilty to second-degree murder of his son after a monthslong investigation that has failed to locate the child's remains.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Disapproval of Trump rises in new Emerson College Polling survey: Political Power Ranker
item.description: President Donald Trump's disapproval rating continued to rise this month in the latest Emerson College Polling survey.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Many swings, few knockout hits as Cuomo and Mamdani trade jabs in heated first NYC mayoral debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo entered Thursday evening’s first New York mayoral debate trying to blunt Democratic front-runner Zohran Mamdani's momentum.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Food aid is safe this month, but shutdown could put it at risk in November
item.description: A federal program that provides food assistance to 40 million low-income people could be at risk in November if the government shutdown isn't resolved by then.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 9 people have now been arrested in connection with the mass shooting in Mississippi, FBI says
item.description: LELAND, Miss. (AP) — Nine people have now been arrested in connection with a mass shooting in the Mississippi Delta town of Leland that left six people dead, an FBI special agent said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Israel identifies remains of 2 more hostages
item.description: Israel’s National Institute of Forensic Medicine on Thursday identified the remains of two more hostages returned from Gaza, as officials and families warned Hamas to hand over the bodies of those still held.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Food assistance is safe through October, but it may be at risk if the shutdown continues
item.description: A federal program that provides food assistance to 40 million low-income people could be at risk in November if the government shutdown isn't resolved by then. And in at least some places, new applications for the program are not being approved.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chief of staff of Yemen Houthi rebel's military dies from wounds suffered in Israeli airstrike
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — An Israeli airstrike targeting the top leaders of Yemen's Houthi rebels in August killed the chief of staff of its military, officials said Thursday, further escalating tensions between the group and Israel even as a ceasefire holds in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Capitol Police called to investigate US flag with swastika in GOP congressional office
item.description: Rep. Dave Taylor (R-Ohio) on Wednesday condemned a symbol on the wall of his Washington, D.C., office, which was reportedly a swastika in the middle of a U.S. flag.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, Fox 5

item.title: Alaska airlifting hundreds from storm-devastated coastal villages
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — One of the most significant airlifts in Alaska history was underway Wednesday to move hundreds of people from coastal villages ravaged by high surf and strong winds from the remnants of Typhoon Halong last weekend, officials said.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mississippi executes a man convicted of raping and killing a college student
item.description: PARCHMAN, Miss. (AP) — A Mississippi man convicted of kidnapping, raping and killing a 20-year-old community college student in 1993 was executed Wednesday. Charles Crawford, 59, was pronounced dead at 6:15 p.m. following a lethal injection at the Mississippi State Penitentiary in Parchman.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Journalists turn in access badges, exit Pentagon rather than agree to new reporting rules
item.description: Dozens of reporters turned in access badges and exited the Pentagon on Wednesday rather than agree to government-imposed restrictions on their work, pushing journalists who cover the American military further from the seat of its power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Smoothie straw leads to arrest in 1984 cold case murder of New York teen
item.description: A straw from a smoothie cup was the evidence that led police in New York to the man they suspect of killing a 16-year-old girl on Long Island in 1984, the Nassau County District Attorney’s Office announced on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, Fox 5

item.title: Jury deliberates in trial of 3 New York corrections officers charged in inmate's fatal beating
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — A jury began deliberating Wednesday in the trial of three former New York state corrections officers charged in the fatal beating of an inmate that was captured on bodycam video.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump and budget chief Vought are making this a government shutdown unlike any other
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is making this government shutdown unlike any the nation has ever seen, giving his budget office rare authority to pick winners and losers — who gets paid or fired, which programs are cut or survive — in an unprecedented restructuring across the federal workf
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Instagram says it's safeguarding teens by limiting them to PG-13 content
item.description: Teenagers on Instagram will be restricted to seeing PG-13 content by default and won't be able to change their settings without a parent's permission, Meta announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Who is President Dallin H. Oaks? A look back at his life before and during his ministry
item.description: The now 18th leader of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints was born on August 12, 1932 in Provo, UT. The Church website says Oaks and his late wife, June Dixon Oaks, had six children.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4
